title: Biological scientists
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 44 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Examine animals to detect and determine the nature of diseases or injuries, and treat sick or injured animals by prescribing medication, setting bones, dressing wounds, or performing surgery. Conduct postmortem studies and analyses to determine the causes of animals' deaths and collect body tissue, feces, blood, urine, or other body fluids for examination and analysis.

Educate the public about diseases that can be spread from animals to humans and provide advice and recommendations to animal owners regarding sanitary measures, feeding, general care, medical conditions, or treatment options.
Train or supervise workers who handle or care for animals and organize, advise, and participate in community activities and organizations, such as county and state fair events and 4-H Clubs.

Direct the overall operations of animal hospitals, clinics, or mobile services to farms and perform administrative or business management tasks, such as scheduling appointments, accepting payments from clients, budgeting, or maintaining business records.
Conduct field demonstrations of new products, techniques, or services and conduct agricultural research, analyze data, and prepare research reports related to animal health and crop management.
Analyze data to determine community program needs and collect and evaluate data related to animal health, productivity, and resource management.

Perform inspections of farms or livestock facilities to ensure compliance with government regulations and inspect and test animals to detect the presence of communicable diseases, establishing or conducting quarantine or testing procedures.

Plan or execute animal nutrition or reproduction programs and advise farmers and demonstrate techniques in areas such as feeding and health maintenance of livestock, growing and harvesting practices, and financial planning.
Establish short- and long-term plans for management of animal health and productivity, as well as oversee activities and performance of animal husbandry workers.
Conduct research diseases to which animals could be susceptible, and determine the effects of drug therapies, antibiotics, or new surgical techniques by testing them on animals.

Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study food processing, safety, and nutritional content; analyze food for levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research methods to make processed foods safe and healthful; and apply food science knowledge for food processing and distribution. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, including pest control and soil composition related to plant growth.

Conduct research on the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals, and study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth.
Determine data collection methods to be employed in research projects or surveys.
Analyze data to determine validity, quality, and scientific significance and to interpret correlations between human activities and environmental effects.
Provide advice on proper standards and regulations or the development of policies, strategies, or codes of practice for environmental management.
Review and implement environmental technical standards, guidelines, policies, and formal regulations that meet all appropriate requirements.
Evaluate violations or problems discovered during inspections to determine appropriate regulatory actions or to provide advice on the development and prosecution of regulatory cases.
Conduct environmental audits, inspections, or investigations of violations, ensuring compliance with environmental or safety policies, standards, or regulations.
Write grants to obtain funding for restoration projects or research, including preparing and reviewing technical and research reports.
Train staff on park programs, promote environmental management systems (EMS), and coordinate public programs including public events and educational outreach related to ecology and conservation.
Conduct field trips to showcase scientific, historic, and natural features and deliver illustrated lectures and interpretive talks about park features.
Manage forage and soil resources, including maintaining soil stability and vegetation for wildlife habitats, outdoor recreation, and monitoring the environmental impact of development activities.
Perform analyses to determine human behavior impacts on environmental changes and develop strategies or methods to minimize environmental impacts of production processes.
Develop environmental management or restoration plans with knowledge of regulations and sustainable practices for natural resources, including habitat management plans.
Research environmental effects of agricultural practices, pollution, and industrial processes to identify mitigation strategies and assess compliance with environmental regulations.
Identify and analyze issues related to food science, environment, and public health, including developing and evaluating food safety standards and regulations.
Investigate diseases and environmental health issues, including conducting research on sustainability related to public health, food safety, or the impact of climate change.
Collect and analyze environmental data related to pollution, ecosystem health, and agricultural productivity, including assessing the effectiveness of ecological practices.
Prepare technical documents for dissemination to stakeholders, including reports, policy recommendations, and scientific publications.
Supervise staff and coordinate efforts with agencies or stakeholders to enhance wildlife and environmental management strategies.
Conduct scientific research, including methodologies for studying living processes, food composition, and environmental impact assessments.
Monitor and evaluate the laboratory techniques related to food and animal sciences and develop improved methods of food preservation, processing, and safety.
Crossbreed animals or study mutations in organisms to identify desirable traits or investigate genetic factors related to health and disease.
Draft and communicate findings from research regardless of the medium, ensuring the accuracy and accessibility of scientific data and its implications for society.
Share research findings by writing scientific articles or by making presentations at scientific conferences.
Manage laboratory teams, supervise biological technicians, technologists, and other scientists, and monitor the quality of a team's work.
Develop and implement new methods to study the mechanisms of biological processes and conduct experiments to investigate underlying mechanisms of plant growth and response to the environment.
Prepare reports and technical recommendations based upon research outcomes, and create or modify web-based bioinformatics tools.
Analyze genetic determinants responsible for specific inherited traits, and devise methods for altering traits or producing new traits in animals and plants.
Use specialized equipment to prepare and analyze organ, tissue, and cell samples; conduct laboratory procedures to identify bacteria, microorganisms, and test for toxicity.

Study the effects of management practices, environmental conditions, and nutrition on the quality and quantity of animal products, and develop improved practices in animal feeding, housing, sanitation, and health control.

Conduct research concerning animal nutrition, breeding, or management to improve products or processes, and advise producers about improved products and techniques that could enhance their animal production efforts.
Study animals in their natural habitats, evaluating the effects of environmental changes and human activities on their behavior, physiology, and populations.
Prepare requests for proposals or statements of work in relation to biological research programs for government or industry.
Examine genetic and biological data by performing appropriate mathematical or statistical calculations and analyses, and create statistical models for the analysis of genetic data.

Conduct applied research aimed at improvements in areas such as disease testing, crop quality, pharmaceuticals, and the harnessing of microbes to recycle waste, and evaluate new technologies to enhance research results.

Collaborate with biologists and other professionals to conduct appropriate genetic and biochemical analyses, and supervise or direct the work of technicians and other personnel applying bioinformatics tools or applications.
Study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical characteristics of soils, and prepare plans for management of renewable resources to improve soil health and crop productivity.
Monitor and report incidents of infectious diseases, and educate healthcare workers, patients, and the public about infectious and communicable diseases.
Identify, classify, and conduct research on plant and animal species, examining their structure, behavior, and ecological relationships.
Research transformations of substances in cells, studying physical principles of living cells or organisms and their interactions with drugs, chemicals, or pathogens.
Develop new products and procedures for sterilization, food and pharmaceutical preservation, or contamination detection; and evaluate, diagnose, or treat genetic disorders.
Study the structure and function of tissues, cells, pathogens, and toxins, as well as the effects of bacteria and microorganisms on living organisms.
Maintain accurate laboratory records and data, following strict safety procedures when handling toxic materials, and supervise technical personnel.
Conduct family medical studies to evaluate the genetic basis for traits or diseases, and engage in educational programs to communicate research findings.
Develop guidelines and protocols for biological procedures, ensuring efficacy and safety in laboratory practices, and verifying equipment and instrumentation is functioning properly.
Integrate bioinformatics into genetic research, including data management and analysis, machine learning applications, and coordinate research activities with interdisciplinary teams.
Review scientific literature and technological advancements to keep abreast of current research findings and innovations in biological sciences and genetics.

Develop ways of altering soils to suit different types of plants, and investigate responses of soils to specific management practices to determine use capabilities of soils and the effects of alternative practices on soil productivity.

Consult with engineers or other technical personnel working on construction projects about the effects of soil problems and possible solutions to these problems, and provide information or recommendations to farmers or other landowners regarding ways to best use land, promote plant growth, or correct problems such as erosion.

Conduct research to determine best methods of planting, spraying, cultivating, harvesting, storing, processing, or transporting horticultural products, and conduct experiments to develop new or improved varieties of field crops, focusing on characteristics such as yield, quality, disease resistance, nutritional value, or adaptation to specific soils or climates.
Develop new or improved methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or insect pests, and develop environmentally safe methods or products for the same purposes.

Provide advice regarding the development of regulatory standards for land reclamation or soil conservation, and develop methods of conserving or managing soil that can be applied by farmers or forestry companies.
Communicate research or project results to other professionals or the public or teach related courses, seminars, or workshops.
Survey undisturbed or disturbed lands for classification, inventory, mapping, environmental impact assessments, environmental protection planning, conservation planning, or reclamation planning.
Plan or supervise waste management programs for composting or farming, and plan or supervise land conservation or reclamation programs for industrial development projects.
Study ways to improve agricultural sustainability, such as the use of new methods of composting, and conduct research into the use of plant species as green fuels or in the production of green fuels.

Investigate soil problems or poor water quality to determine sources and effects, and identify degraded or contaminated soils and develop plans to improve their chemical, biological, or physical characteristics.

Study soil characteristics to classify soils on the basis of factors such as geographic location, landscape position, or soil properties, and develop improved measurement techniques, soil conservation methods, soil sampling devices, or related technology.
Conduct experiments investigating how soil forms, changes, or interacts with land-based ecosystems or living organisms.
Conduct experiments regarding causes of bee diseases or factors affecting yields of nectar or pollen.
